name: Homemade Buttery Herb Biscuits
intro: Sunday morning at my grandma’s house—this is what these biscuits remind me of. They are rustic, they are warm, they are herby, they’re buttery and they just feel like home. This is the recipe you need if you’re into southern-style cooking! I would pair this with my Not-So-Southern Sausage Gravy, for some real southern flare.

ingredients.item: all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting

method: Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.

Start by making your herby vegan compound butter for the biscuits: In a bowl, combine your butter with the garlic paste, oregano, parsley and a pinch of salt. Make sure everything is very well mixed. Once you combine the ingredients, mold the butter back into a log, wrap with plastic wrap and put it in the freezer. This is important—we need cold butter to make buttery, flaky biscuits. Let it get really cold before using it in the dough. This takes about an hour.
In a small bowl or measuring cup, combine your milk and apple cider vinegar to create your vegan buttermilk. Set aside so it can curdle while you make the dough.
In a food processor, combine the flour, baking powder and sugar and pulse to mix.

Now, cut the compound butter into cubes, add to the flour mixture and pulse a couple of times to combine. I like to use the food processor for this. Done traditionally, where you use your hands, the heat from your hands starts to melt the butter, but you want the butter to stay cold. Pulse until you get course crumbles, then pour the dough into a large bowl.

Scoop the dough onto a lightly floured surface and gently work it together with your hands, shaping it into a rectangle. Flour your hands to prevent sticking and sprinkle a little flour on the dough if it’s very sticky; just don’t overdo it with the flour because you don’t want the biscuits to be dry. Continue to flatten the dough into a larger rectangle, then fold one short side toward the center, then the other short side toward the center as well, atop the other folded portion, like folding a letter. Turn the dough by 90 degrees and flatten it again into a rectangle, then fold it into itself in thirds. Repeat the process about three more times. This is what makes the layers in the biscuits.

Using a biscuit cutter, cut out as many biscuits as you can. Continue to fold the dough and cut out biscuits until there is no more dough left. Arrange the biscuits about ½ inch (1.3 cm) apart on the baking sheet and bake for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden brown on top and they have risen a bit. Make sure you do not overbake these, because they will become hard.
If you want, make an extra portion of compound butter for serving. Eat these biscuits with herby compound butter, jam or with the Not-So-Southern Sausage Gravy.
